en_question: Which of the following are advantages of moving to the AWS Cloud? (Choose two.)

en_options.A: The ability to turn over the responsibility for all security to AWS.
en_options.B: The ability to use the pay-as-you-go model.
en_options.C: The ability to have full control over the physical infrastructure.
en_options.D: No longer having to guess what capacity will be required.
en_options.E: No longer worrying about users access controls.

comments: B. The ability to use the pay-as-you-go model - AWS provides a flexible and cost-effective pay-as-you-go pricing model, allowing users to pay only for the resources they consume without upfront costs or long-term commitments. D. No longer having to guess what capacity will be required - AWS offers scalable resources, enabling users to dynamically scale up or down based on their actual needs. This eliminates the need for upfront capacity planning and allows for efficient resource utilization.
B. 按需付费模式——AWS 提供灵活且经济高效的按需付费定价模式，用户只需为实际使用的资源付费，无需 预付费用或长期承诺。D. 无需再猜测所需容量——AWS 提供可扩展资源，使用户能够根据实际需求动态扩展或缩减。 这消除了预先进行容量规划的需要，并实现了高效的资源利用。

cn_question: AWS 提供的哪项服务是混合云存储服务，可为本地用户提供几乎无限的云存储空间？
en_question: Which AWS service is a hybrid cloud storage service that provides on-premises users access to virtually unlimited cloud storage?
comments: A. AWS DataSync: A data transfer service that simplifies, automates, and accelerates moving large amounts of data between on-premises storage systems and AWS Cloud storage. It is focused on efficient data transfer. B. Amazon S3 Glacier: A storage service designed for archiving and long-term backup of infrequently accessed data. It is suitable for data that is rarely accessed and requires low-cost storage. While it is a cloud storage service, it is not specifically designed for on-premises users to access virtually unlimited cloud storage. C. AWS Storage Gateway: A hybrid cloud storage service that enables on-premises applications to use cloud storage seamlessly. It provides file, volume, and tape gateway interfaces to integrate on-premises environments with AWS Cloud storage. This service allows on-premises users to access virtually unlimited cloud storage while maintaining a hybrid storage infrastructure. D. Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS): Provides block-level storage volumes for use with Amazon EC2 instances. It is primarily focused on providing scalable and high-performance block storage for EC2 instances.

C. AWS Storage Gateway: A hybrid cloud storage service that enables on-premises applications to use cloud storage seamlessly. It provides file, volume, and tape gateway interfaces to integrate on-premises environments with AWS Cloud storage. This service allows on-premises users to access virtually unlimited cloud storage while maintaining a hybrid storage infrastructure.

cn_question: 一家公司计划迁移到 AWS，并希望为其 AWS 使用案例创建成本估算。 该公司可以使用哪些 AWS 服务或工具来满足这些要求？
en_question: A company plans to migrate to AWS and wants to create cost estimates for its AWS use cases. Which AWS service or tool can the company use to meet these requirements?
comments: A. AWS Pricing Calculator: A web-based tool that allows users to estimate the cost of using AWS services. It helps in understanding and estimating the costs associated with various AWS resources based on usage patterns, regions, and other parameters. Users can input their specific requirements to get an estimated monthly cost. B. Amazon CloudWatch: A monitoring and observability service for AWS resources. C. AWS Cost Explorer: A tool within the AWS Management Console that provides visualization and analysis of AWS costs and usage. It allows users to view, understand, and analyze their historical AWS costs and usage data. While it provides insights into existing costs, it is not primarily a tool for creating initial cost estimates. D. AWS Budgets: A service that allows users to set custom cost and usage budgets that alert them when they exceed their thresholds. It helps in managing costs by providing notifications based on cost and usage performance against defined budget targets. While it helps in budgeting and monitoring, it may not be the primary tool for creating detailed initial cost estimates.
A. Calculadora de precios de AWS

A. AWS 定价计算器：一款基于 Web 的工具，允许用户估算使用 AWS 服务的成本。它有助于用户根据使用 模式、区域和其他参数了解和估算各种 AWS 资源的相关成本。用户可以输入具体需求以获得预估的月度成本。B. Amazon CloudWatch：一项用于监控和可观测性的 AWS 资源服务。C. AWS Cost Explorer：AWS 管理控制台中的 一个工具，提供 AWS 成本和使用情况的可视化和分析。它允许用户查看、了解和分析其历史 AWS 成本和使用情况数 据。虽然它提供了对现有成本的洞察，但它并非主要用于创建初始成本估算。D. AWS Budgets：一项服务，允许用户 设置自定义成本和使用预算，并在超出阈值时发出警报。它通过根据成本和使用情况与已定义预算目标的对比情况提供 通知来帮助管理成本。虽然它有助于预算和监控，但它可能不是创建详细初始成本估算的主要工具。
A. AWS 定价计算器：这是一款基于 Web 的工具，可帮助用户估算使用 AWS 服务的成本。它能根据使用模 式、区域和其他参数，帮助用户了解和估算各种 AWS 资源的相关成本。用户可以输入具体需求，以获得预估的每月费 用。

cn_question: 开发人员应该使用哪种工具将 AWS 服务功能直接集成到应用程序中？
en_question: Which tool should a developer use to integrate AWS service features directly into an application?
comments: A. AWS Software Development Kit (SDK): A set of libraries and tools that allows developers to interact with AWS services directly from their applications. It provides APIs in various programming languages, enabling developers to integrate AWS services seamlessly into their applications. It abstracts the complexity of making direct API calls and provides a convenient way to interact with AWS resources. B. AWS CodeDeploy: A service that automates code deployments to Amazon EC2 instances, on-premises instances, or serverless Lambda functions. C. AWS Lambda: A serverless compute service that lets developers run code without provisioning or managing servers. Developers can use Lambda to execute code in response to events, such as changes to data in an Amazon S3 bucket or an update to a DynamoDB table. D. AWS Batch: Enables developers to run batch computing workloads on the AWS Cloud. It allows users to define and run batch computing jobs efficiently.

A. AWS Software Development Kit (SDK): A set of libraries and tools that allows developers to interact with AWS services directly from their applications. It provides APIs in various programming languages, enabling developers to integrate AWS services seamlessly into their applications. It abstracts the complexity of making direct API calls and provides a convenient way to interact with AWS resources.

A. AWS 软件开发工具包 (SDK)：一套库和工具，允许开发人员直接从其应用程序与 AWS 服务交互。它提供 多种编程语言的 API，使开发人员能够将 AWS 服务无缝集成到其应用程序中。它抽象了直接调用 API 的复杂性，并提 供了一种便捷的方式来与 AWS 资源交互。B. AWS CodeDeploy：一项可自动将代码部署到 Amazon EC2 实例、本地 实例或无服务器 Lambda 函数的服务。C. AWS Lambda：一项无服务器计算服务，允许开发人员在无需预置或管理服 务器的情况下运行代码。开发人员可以使用 Lambda 来响应事件（例如 Amazon S3 存储桶中的数据更改或 DynamoDB 表的更新）来执行代码。D. AWS Batch：使开发人员能够在 AWS 云上运行批处理计算工作负载。它允许 用户高效地定义和运行批处理计算作业。

cn_question: 以下哪项是 AWS 良好架构框架推荐的设计原则？

cn_options.A: 减少基础设施变更的频率，并每次变更的幅度要大，从而减少停机时间。
cn_options.B: 花时间手动配置基础设施。
cn_options.C: 从运营失败中吸取教训，不断改进。
cn_options.D: 采用单体应用设计实现集中化。

en_question: Which of the following is a recommended design principle of the AWS Well-Architected Framework?

en_options.A: Reduce downtime by making infrastructure changes infrequently and in large increments.
en_options.B: Invest the time to configure infrastructure manually.
en_options.C: Learn to improve from operational failures.
en_options.D: Use monolithic application design for centralization.

comments: A. Reduce downtime by making infrastructure changes infrequently and in large increments: This statement goes against the best practices recommended by the AWS Well-Architected Framework. It's generally recommended to make frequent and smaller changes to infrastructure to reduce the risk of issues and improve overall system agility. B. Invest the time to configure infrastructure manually: The AWS Well-Architected Framework encourages the use of automation to configure and manage infrastructure. Manual configuration is prone to errors, less scalable, and harder to maintain compared to automated approaches. C. Learn to improve from operational failures: This is a key principle of the AWS Well-Architected Framework. It emphasizes the importance of learning from failures and continuously improving the architecture based on operational experiences. This involves implementing mechanisms for monitoring, logging, and analyzing failures to enhance system resilience. D. Use monolithic application design for centralization: The AWS Well-Architected Framework generally favors a microservices architecture over a monolithic design. Microservices promote modularity, scalability, and flexibility.

C. Learn to improve from operational failures: This is a key principle of the AWS Well-Architected Framework. It emphasizes the importance of learning from failures and continuously improving the architecture based on operational experiences. This involves implementing mechanisms for monitoring, logging, and analyzing failures to enhance system resilience.

C. Learn to improve from operational failures: This is a key principle of the AWS Well-Architected Framework. It emphasizes the importance of learning from failures and continuously improving the architecture based on operational experiences. This involves implementing mechanisms for monitoring, logging, and analyzing failures to enhance system resilience.

A. 减少基础设施变更频率，每次变更幅度较大，从而降低停机时间：这种说法与 AWS 架构完善框架推荐的 最佳实践相悖。通常建议频繁地对基础设施进行小幅变更，以降低问题风险并提高系统整体敏捷性。B. 投入时间手动配 置基础设施：AWS 架构完善框架鼓励使用自动化来配置和管理基础设施。与自动化方法相比，手动配置更容易出错， 可扩展性较差，且更难维护。C. 从运营失败中吸取经验教训：这是 AWS 架构完善框架的关键原则。它强调从失败中学 习并根据运营经验持续改进架构的重要性。这包括实施监控、日志记录和故障分析机制，以增强系统弹性。D. 使用单 体应用设计实现集中化：AWS 架构完善框架通常更倾向于微服务架构而非单体设计。微服务架构具有模块化、可扩展 性和灵活性等优点。
C. 从运营失败中吸取教训并加以改进：这是 AWS 架构完善框架的关键原则之一。它强调从失败中学习并根 据运营经验持续改进架构的重要性。这包括实施监控、记录和分析故障的机制，以增强系统弹性。

cn_question: 使用 AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) 仅授予执行任务所需的资源访问权限的概念称为：
en_question: Using AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) to grant access only to the resources needed to perform a task is a concept known as:
comments: C. Least privilege access: Least privilege access means granting users or entities the minimum level of permissions required to perform their tasks, reducing the risk of unintended or malicious actions.

A. Restricted access: While the term "restricted access" is used in the context of limiting access, it is not a specific concept associated with IAM in AWS. It doesn't convey the principle of granting the minimum necessary permissions for a task. B. As-needed access: This term is not a standard concept associated with IAM. However, it aligns somewhat with the idea of granting access based on specific needs. C. Least privilege access: Least privilege access means granting users or entities the minimum level of permissions required to perform their tasks, reducing the risk of unintended or malicious actions. D. Token access: "Token access" is not a standard term in the context of AWS IAM. Tokens are often associated with authentication and authorization, but the specific concept of least privilege access is better described by option C.
的风险。 C. 最小权限访问：最小权限访问是指授予用户或实体执行其任务所需的最低权限，从而降低意外或恶意行为

A. 受限访问：虽然“受限访问”一词用于限制访问权限，但它并非 AWS IAM 的特定概念。它并未体现授予任 务所需最小权限的原则。B. 按需访问：该术语并非 IAM 的标准概念。然而，它与根据特定需求授予访问权限的理念在 某种程度上是一致的。C. 最小权限访问：最小权限访问是指授予用户或实体执行其任务所需的最小权限，从而降低意 外或恶意操作的风险。D. 令牌访问：“令牌访问”并非 AWS IAM 的标准术语。令牌通常与身份验证和授权相关联，但最 小权限访问的具体概念在选项 C 中描述得更为准确。

en_question: A company wants to operate a data warehouse to analyze data without managing the data warehouse infrastructure. Which AWS service will meet this requirement?
comments: A. Amazon Aurora: A relational database engine offered as part of Amazon RDS (Relational Database Service). While it is a high-performance database engine, it is optimized for transactional workloads rather than analytical processing typical of data warehouses. B. Amazon Redshift: A fully managed, petabyte-scale data warehouse service in the cloud. It is specifically designed for analytics and data warehousing, offering fast query performance using SQL queries and integration with various business intelligence tools. C. AWS Lambda: A serverless compute service that allows you to run code without provisioning or managing servers. It is event-driven and primarily used for executing code in response to events, such as changes in data or system state. D. Amazon RDS: A managed relational database service that supports various database engines like MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQL Server, and MariaDB. Similar to Amazon Aurora, it is optimized for transactional workloads rather than analytical processing.

B. Amazon Redshift: A fully managed, petabyte-scale data warehouse service in the cloud. It is specifically designed for analytics and data warehousing, offering fast query performance using SQL queries and integration with various business intelligence tools.
B. Amazon Redshift Serverless - key words "data warehouse"

A. Amazon Aurora：作为 Amazon RDS（关系数据库服务）的一部分提供的关系数据库引擎。虽然它是一款 高性能数据库引擎，但它针对事务性工作负载进行了优化，而非数据仓库中常见的分析处理。B. Amazon Redshift：完 全托管的云端 PB 级数据仓库服务。它专为分析和数据仓库而设计，使用 SQL 查询提供快速查询性能，并可与各种商 业智能工具集成。C. AWS Lambda：一种无服务器计算服务，允许您在无需预置或管理服务器的情况下运行代码。它 是事件驱动的，主要用于响应事件（例如数据或系统状态的更改）来执行代码。D. Amazon RDS：一种托管关系数据 库服务，支持各种数据库引擎，例如 MySQL、PostgreSQL、Oracle、SQL Server 和 MariaDB。与 Amazon Aurora 类似，它针对事务性工作负载进行了优化，而非分析处理。
B. Amazon Redshift：一款完全托管的云端PB级数据仓库服务。它专为分析和数据仓库而设计，利用SQL查 询提供快速的查询性能，并可与各种商业智能工具集成。

cn_question: AWS云计算如何帮助企业降低成本？（选择两项。）

cn_options.A: AWS 在每个 AWS 区域中对服务收取相同的价格。
cn_options.B: AWS 允许按需调整容量。
cn_options.C: AWS 为闲置超过 1 周的 Amazon EC2 实例提供折扣。
cn_options.D: AWS 不会对从 AWS 云发送到互联网的数据收取费用。
cn_options.E: AWS 消除了构建和维护本地数据中心的许多成本。

en_question: How does AWS Cloud computing help businesses reduce costs? (Choose two.)

en_options.A: AWS charges the same prices for services in every AWS Region.
en_options.B: AWS enables capacity to be adjusted on demand.
en_options.C: AWS offers discounts for Amazon EC2 instances that remain idle for more than 1 week.
en_options.D: AWS does not charge for data sent from the AWS Cloud to the internet.
en_options.E: AWS eliminates many of the costs of building and maintaining on-premises data centers. Most Voted

comments: B. AWS enables capacity to be adjusted on demand: AWS provides the flexibility to scale resources up or down based on demand. This allows businesses to optimize costs by only paying for the resources they actually use, avoiding unnecessary expenses during periods of lower demand. E. AWS eliminates many of the costs of building and maintaining on-premises data centers: With AWS, businesses can leverage cloud infrastructure without the need to invest in and maintain physical data centers. This eliminates upfront capital expenses, ongoing maintenance costs, and the need to overprovision resources for future growth, leading to significant cost savings.

Answer: BE Data transfer between AWS and internet  There is no charge for inbound data transfer across all services in all Regions. Data transfer from AWS to the internet is charged per service, with rates specific to the originating Region. Refer to the pricing pages for each service—for example, the pricing page for Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2)—for more details.
B. AWS 支持按需调整容量：AWS 可根据需求灵活扩展或缩减资源。这使得企业只需为实际使用的资源付 费，从而优化成本，避免在需求低谷期产生不必要的支出。E. AWS 消除了构建和维护本地数据中心的诸多成本：借助 AWS，企业无需投资和维护物理数据中心即可利用云基础设施。这省去了前期资本支出、持续维护成本以及为未来增长 过度配置资源的需求，从而显著节省成本。

答：AWS 与互联网之间的数据传输 所有区域的所有服务之间的入站数据传输均免费。从 AWS 到互联网的数 据传输按服务收费，费率取决于源区域。有关更多详细信息，请参阅各项服务的定价页面，例如 Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) 的定价页面。https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/architecture/overview-of-datatransfer-costs-for-common-architectures/

cn_question: 一家公司希望授予一个 AWS 账户中的用户访问另一个 AWS 账户中资源的权限。这些用户目前没有访问这些资源 的权限。 哪项 AWS 服务可以满足此要求？
en_question: A company wants to grant users in one AWS account access to resources in another AWS account. The users do not currently have permission to access the resources. Which AWS service will meet this requirement?
comments: A. IAM group: Containers for IAM users. They are used to simplify the management of IAM policies by allowing you to attach policies to a group and automatically apply those policies to all users in the group. However, IAM groups are not directly used for cross-account access. B. IAM role: Are used to delegate permissions to users, applications, or services. In the context of crossaccount access, you can create an IAM role in the target account and define policies that grant access to the necessary resources. Users in the source account can assume the role to access resources in the target account. IAM roles are commonly used for cross-account access scenarios. C. IAM tag: Are metadata that you can assign to IAM users, groups, roles, and policies. While tags are useful for organizing and managing resources, they are not the primary mechanism for granting cross-account access. D. IAM Access Analyzer: A tool that helps identify resources that are shared with an external entity or are publicly accessible. It is used for analyzing access across accounts, but not specifically for setting up crossaccount access.

B. IAM role: Are used to delegate permissions to users, applications, or services. In the context of crossaccount access, you can create an IAM role in the target account and define policies that grant access to the necessary resources. Users in the source account can assume the role to access resources in the target account. IAM roles are commonly used for cross-account access scenarios.
B. IAM role

A. IAM 组：IAM 用户的容器。它们用于简化 IAM 策略的管理，允许您将策略附加到组，并自动将这些策略应 用于组中的所有用户。但是，IAM 组并不直接用于跨账户访问。B. IAM 角色：用于将权限委派给用户、应用程序或服 务。在跨账户访问的上下文中，您可以在目标账户中创建 IAM 角色，并定义授予对必要资源访问权限的策略。源账户 中的用户可以承担该角色以访问目标账户中的资源。IAM 角色通常用于跨账户访问场景。C. IAM 标签：您可以将其分 配给 IAM 用户、组、角色和策略的元数据。虽然标签对于组织和管理资源很有用，但它们并非授予跨账户访问的主要 机制。D. IAM 访问分析器：一种帮助识别与外部实体共享或可公开访问的资源的工具。它用于分析跨账户的访问权 限，但并非专门用于设置跨账户访问。
B. IAM 角色：用于向用户、应用程序或服务委派权限。在跨账户访问的场景中，您可以在目标账户中创建 IAM 角色，并定义授予其访问必要资源的策略。源账户中的用户可以担任该角色来访问目标账户中的资源。IAM 角色通 常用于跨账户访问场景。

cn_question: 一家公司希望使用基础设施即代码 (IaC) 实现基础设施部署自动化。该公司希望扩展生产环境堆栈，以便将这些 堆栈部署到多个 AWS 区域。 哪项 AWS 服务能够满足这些要求？
en_question: A company wants to automate infrastructure deployment by using infrastructure as code (IaC). The company wants to scale production stacks so the stacks can be deployed in multiple AWS Regions. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
comments: "AWS CloudFormation gives you an easy way to model a collection of related AWS and third-party resources, provision them quickly and consistently, and manage them throughout their lifecycles, by treating infrastructure as code. A CloudFormation template describes your desired resources and their dependencies so you can launch and configure them together as a stack. You can use a template to create, update, and delete an entire stack as a single unit, as often as you need to, instead of managing resources individually. You can manage and provision stacks across multiple AWS accounts and AWS Regions." Reference: https://aws.amazon.com/cloudformation/features/

A. Amazon CloudWatch: A monitoring service for AWS resources. It allows you to collect and track metrics, collect and monitor log files, and set alarms. B. AWS Config: A service that provides a detailed inventory of your AWS resources and their configurations, as well as configuration history. It helps you assess, audit, and evaluate the configurations of your AWS resources. C. AWS Trusted Advisor: A service that provides recommendations to help optimize your AWS infrastructure for cost efficiency, performance, security, and fault tolerance. It offers best practices guidance. D. AWS CloudFormation: A service that allows you to define and provision AWS infrastructure as code. You can use CloudFormation templates to describe the resources needed, and CloudFormation handles the  provisioning and deployment. It supports the automated deployment and scaling of infrastructure stacks across multiple AWS Regions, making it suitable for IaC and scaling production stacks.

D. AWS CloudFormation: A service that allows you to define and provision AWS infrastructure as code. You can use CloudFormation templates to describe the resources needed, and CloudFormation handles the provisioning and deployment. It supports the automated deployment and scaling of infrastructure stacks across multiple AWS Regions, making it suitable for IaC and scaling production stacks.

AWS CloudFormation 提供了一种简便的方法来建模一组相关的 AWS 和第三方资源，快速、一致地配置这 些资源，并在其整个生命周期内进行管理，将基础设施视为代码。CloudFormation 模板描述了您所需的资源及其依赖 关系，以便您可以将它们作为一个堆栈一起启动和配置。您可以根据需要使用模板来创建、更新和删除整个堆栈，而无 需单独管理资源。您可以跨多个 AWS 账户和 AWS 区域管理和配置堆栈。参考：

A. Amazon CloudWatch：一项用于监控 AWS 资源的服务。它允许您收集和跟踪指标、收集和监控日志文件 以及设置警报。B. AWS Config：一项提供 AWS 资源及其配置的详细清单以及配置历史记录的服务。它可帮助您评 估、审核和验证 AWS 资源的配置。C. AWS Trusted Advisor：一项提供建议的服务，可帮助您优化 AWS 基础设施， 以提高成本效益、性能、安全性和容错能力。它提供最佳实践指南。D. AWS CloudFormation：一项允许您以代码形 式定义和配置 AWS 基础设施的服务。您可以使用 CloudFormation 模板来描述所需的资源，CloudFormation 会处理 配置和部署。它支持跨多个 AWS 区域自动部署和扩展基础设施堆栈，使其适用于 IaC 和扩展生产堆栈。

cn_question: 一家公司使用第三方服务将 10 TB 的数据备份到磁带库。其本地备份服务器空间不足。该公司希望使用 AWS 服 务进行备份，同时保持现有的备份工作流程不变。 为了满足这些要求，该公司应该使用哪项 AWS 服务？
en_question: A company is using a third-party service to back up 10 TB of data to a tape library. The on-premises backup server is running out of space. The company wants to use AWS services for the backups without changing its existing backup workflows. Which AWS service should the company use to meet these requirements?
comments: A. Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS): This service provides block-level storage volumes primarily used with Amazon EC2 instances. It may not be the best fit for backup scenarios involving tape libraries. B. AWS Storage Gateway: This is a hybrid cloud storage service that seamlessly integrates on-premises applications with cloud storage. It supports various storage protocols, including Amazon S3 and Amazon Glacier, and allows on-premises data to be backed up to AWS without changing existing workflows. C. Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS): This service is for container orchestration and managing containerized applications. It is not directly related to back up scenarios or large-scale data storage. D. AWS Lambda: This is a serverless compute service for running code in response to events.

B. AWS Storage Gateway: This is a hybrid cloud storage service that seamlessly integrates on-premises applications with cloud storage. It supports various storage protocols, including Amazon S3 and Amazon Glacier, and allows on-premises data to be backed up to AWS without changing existing workflows.
B AWS Storage Gateway
B. AWS Storage Gateway：这是一种混合云存储服务，可将本地应用程序与云存储无缝集成。它支持多种存 储协议，包括 Amazon S3 和 Amazon Glacier，并允许将本地数据备份到 AWS，而无需更改现有工作流程。

Reserved Instance Reporting: Provides insights into the utilization and coverage of Reserved Instances, helping users understand how effectively Reserved Instances are utilized.

cn_question: 根据AWS责任共担模型，哪些任务是客户的责任？（选择两项。）

cn_options.E: 修补 Amazon RDS 数据库实例。

en_question: ’ Which tasks are the customer s responsibility, according to the AWS shared responsibility model? (Choose two.)
comments: Perform client-side data encryption: This task is the responsibility of the customer to encrypt data on the client side before sending it to AWS. It ensures that data is secure during transit. Configure IAM credentials: Customers are responsible for configuring Identity and Access Management (IAM) credentials. This includes managing user accounts, permissions, and authentication mechanisms for accessing AWS resources. Establish the global infrastructure: This is typically a task managed by AWS. AWS is responsible for setting up and managing the global infrastructure, including data centers and the underlying network. Secure edge locations: AWS is responsible for securing its edge locations. This includes ensuring the physical security and proper operation of these locations. Patch Amazon RDS DB instances: This is typically a task managed by AWS. AWS is responsible for applying updates and security patches to Amazon RDS DB instances to keep them up to date and secure. Customers are responsible for their data and configurations within the RDS instances.
B. Perform client-side data encryption. C. Configure IAM credentials.

Perform client-side data encryption: This task is the responsibility of the customer to encrypt data on the client side before sending it to AWS. It ensures that data is secure during transit. Configure IAM credentials: Customers are responsible for configuring Identity and Access Management (IAM) credentials. This includes managing user accounts, permissions, and authentication mechanisms for accessing AWS resources.

执行客户端数据加密：客户负责在客户端对数据进行加密，然后再将其发送到 AWS。这可确保数据在传输过 程中的安全。配置 IAM 凭证：客户负责配置身份和访问管理 (IAM) 凭证。这包括管理用户帐户、权限以及访问 AWS 资源的身份验证机制。建立全球基础设施：这通常由 AWS 管理。AWS 负责设置和管理全球基础设施，包括数据中心 和底层网络。保护边缘站点：AWS 负责保护其边缘站点。这包括确保这些站点的物理安全和正常运行。修补 Amazon RDS 数据库实例：这通常由 AWS 管理。AWS 负责将更新和安全补丁应用到 Amazon RDS 数据库实例，以保持其最新 和安全。客户负责其 RDS 实例中的数据和配置。
B. 执行客户端数据加密。C. 配置 IAM 凭证。

en_question: A developer has been hired by a large company and needs AWS credentials. Which are security best practices that should be followed? (Choose two.) ’ ’

en_options.A: Grant the developer access to only the AWS resources needed to perform the job.
en_options.B: Share the AWS account root user credentials with the developer.
en_options.C: Add the developer to the administrator s group in AWS IAM.
en_options.D: Configure a password policy that ensures the developer s password cannot be changed.
en_options.E: Ensure the account password policy requires a minimum length.

comments: A. Grant the developer access to only the AWS resources needed to perform the job: Following the principle of least privilege, it is advisable to provide the developer with access only to the specific AWS resources necessary for their job role. This minimizes the potential impact of security incidents and limits the scope of actions the developer can perform. E. Ensure the account password policy requires a minimum length: Implementing a password policy that requires a minimum length is a good security practice. It helps enhance the strength of passwords and contributes to better overall account security. Longer passwords are generally more resistant to brute-force attacks.

A. Grant the developer access to only the AWS resources needed to perform the job: Following the principle of least privilege, it is advisable to provide the developer with access only to the specific AWS resources necessary for their job role. This minimizes the potential impact of security incidents and limits the scope of actions the developer can perform. E. Ensure the account password policy requires a minimum length: Implementing a password policy that requires a minimum length is a good security practice. It helps enhance the strength of passwords and contributes to better overall account security. Longer passwords are generally more resistant to brute-force attacks. Options B and C and D are not recommended
A. 仅授予开发人员执行工作所需的 AWS 资源访问权限：遵循最小权限原则，建议仅向开发人员提供其工作 角色所需的特定 AWS 资源访问权限。这可以最大限度地减少安全事件的潜在影响，并限制开发人员可以执行的操作范 围。E. 确保账户密码策略要求最小长度：实施要求最小长度的密码策略是一种良好的安全实践。它有助于增强密码强 度，并有助于提高整体账户安全性。较长的密码通常更能抵御暴力破解攻击。

en_question: A user wants to allow applications running on an Amazon EC2 instance to make calls to other AWS services. The access granted must be secure. Which AWS service or feature should be used?
comments: For allowing applications running on an Amazon EC2 instance to make secure calls to other AWS services, the recommended AWS service or feature is: C. IAM roles IAM (Identity and Access Management) roles provide a secure way to grant permissions to AWS services and resources. In this scenario, you can create an IAM role with the necessary permissions for the EC2 instance to access other AWS services. Then, you can associate the IAM role with the EC2 instance. Option A (Security groups) is used for controlling inbound and outbound traffic to and from an EC2 instance, but it's not directly related to granting permissions to AWS services.

C. IAM roles: Are used to grant secure and temporary access to AWS services. In this scenario, where a user wants to allow applications running on an Amazon EC2 instance to make calls to other AWS services, IAM roles should be used. IAM roles provide a secure way to delegate permissions to entities like EC2 instances without the need for long-term credentials. Option A (Security groups) is used for controlling inbound and outbound traffic to EC2 instances but does not provide secure access to AWS services. Option B (AWS Firewall Manager) is a service used for managing AWS WAF (Web Application Firewall) rules across accounts and applications, and it is not directly related to granting permissions to EC2 instances.  Option D (IAM user SSH keys) is specifically related to SSH key pairs for IAM users and is not the appropriate solution for granting access to AWS services from EC2 instances.

C. IAM roles IAM (Identity and Access Management) roles provide a secure way to grant permissions to AWS services and resources. In this scenario, you can create an IAM role with the necessary permissions for the EC2 instance to access other AWS services. Then, you can associate the IAM role with the EC2 instance.

要允许运行在 Amazon EC2 实例上的应用程序安全地调用其他 AWS 服务，建议使用以下 AWS 服务或功 能：C. IAM 角色。IAM（身份和访问管理）角色提供了一种安全的方式来授予对 AWS 服务和资源的权限。在这种情况 下，您可以创建一个具有 EC2 实例访问其他 AWS 服务所需权限的 IAM 角色。然后，您可以将该 IAM 角色与 EC2 实 例关联。选项 A（安全组）用于控制进出 EC2 实例的流量，但它与授予对 AWS 服务的权限没有直接关系。

C. IAM 角色：用于授予对 AWS 服务的安全临时访问权限。在这种情况下，如果用户希望允许运行在 Amazon EC2 实例上的应用程序调用其他 AWS 服务，则应使用 IAM 角色。IAM 角色提供了一种安全的方式，可以将 权限委派给 EC2 实例等实体，而无需长期凭证。选项 A（安全组）用于控制进出 EC2 实例的流量，但不能提供对 AWS 服务的安全访问。选项 B（AWS 防火墙管理器）是一项用于跨账户和应用程序管理 AWS WAF（Web 应用程序 防火墙）规则的服务，与授予 EC2 实例权限没有直接关系。选项 D（IAM 用户 SSH 密钥）专门针对 IAM 用户的 SSH 密钥对，并非从 EC2 实例授予 AWS 服务访问权限的合适解决方案。

cn_question: 一家公司需要一台完全托管的 Windows 文件服务器来运行其基于 Windows 的应用程序。 哪项 AWS 服务可以满足此需求？
en_question: A company wants a fully managed Windows file server for its Windows-based applications. Which AWS service will meet this requirement?
comments: A. Amazon FSx: A fully managed file storage service that is compatible with Windows file servers. It is designed to provide shared file storage for Windows-based applications, making it a suitable choice for the company's requirement of a fully managed Windows file server. Option B (Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service - Amazon EKS) and Option C (Amazon Elastic Container Service - Amazon ECS) are container orchestration services, not file storage services. Option D (Amazon EMR) is Amazon Elastic MapReduce, which is a cloud-based big data platform, and it is not specifically designed for fully managed Windows file servers.

Answer is A because: Service Primary purpose Support for Windows file servers Amazon FSx Managed file storage Yes Amazon EKS Managed Kubernetes service No Amazon ECS Managed container orchestration service No Amazon EMR Managed Hadoop and Spark service No

A. Amazon FSx：一项完全托管的文件存储服务，与 Windows 文件服务器兼容。它旨在为基于 Windows 的 应用程序提供共享文件存储，因此非常适合公司对完全托管的 Windows 文件服务器的需求。选项 B（Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service - Amazon EKS）和选项 C（Amazon Elastic Container Service - Amazon ECS）是容器编排服 务，而非文件存储服务。选项 D（Amazon EMR）是 Amazon Elastic MapReduce，这是一个基于云的大数据平台， 并非专门为完全托管的 Windows 文件服务器而设计。
答案是 A，因为：服务主要用途：支持 Windows 文件服务器；Amazon FSx 托管文件存储：是；Amazon EKS 托管 Kubernetes 服务：否；Amazon ECS 托管容器编排服务：否；Amazon EMR 托管 Hadoop 和 Spark 服 务：否

cn_question: 一家公司希望将其本地 NFS 工作负载迁移到 AWS。 该公司应该使用哪种 AWS Storage Gateway 类型来满足此要求？
en_question: A company wants to migrate its NFS on-premises workload to AWS. Which AWS Storage Gateway type should the company use to meet this requirement?
comments: I think this question is a little bit out of scope for this exam, but the AWS S3 File Gateway documentation specifically mentions NFS. You probably could also do C, but I think D is the "Best" answer A. Tape Gateway: Is used for archiving data to Amazon S3 and Glacier. It is not designed for NFS workloads or migrating on-premises file-based workloads. B. Volume Gateway: Is used for block storage volumes and might not be the most suitable option for NFS file workloads. It's more focused on block storage solutions. C. Amazon FSx File Gateway: Provides a file interface to Amazon FSx file systems, which is compatible with Windows file servers. However, it may not be the optimal solution for migrating NFS workloads to AWS. D. Amazon S3 File Gateway: Supports a file interface into Amazon S3 and allows storing and retrieving objects in Amazon S3 using industry-standard file protocols such as NFS. It is designed for scenarios where you want to integrate on-premises file-based applications with Amazon S3, making it suitable for migrating NFS workloads to AWS.

D. Amazon S3 File Gateway: Supports a file interface into Amazon S3 and allows storing and retrieving objects in Amazon S3 using industry-standard file protocols such as NFS. It is designed for scenarios where you want to integrate on-premises file-based applications with Amazon S3, making it suitable for migrating NFS workloads to AWS.

If the company is migrating an NFS-based workload (Network File System) from on-premises to AWS, Amazon FSx File Gateway is the best option. It is specifically designed to provide on-premises access to Amazon FSx for Windows File Server or Amazon FSx for Lustre file systems, which can support NFS protocols for file-based workloads. Amazon FSx File Gateway enables seamless access to file data stored in Amazon FSx file systems via standard file protocols like NFS, which is ideal for companies migrating NFS workloads. D. Amazon S3 File Gateway Amazon S3 File Gateway provides access to Amazon S3 via file protocols like NFS and SMB, but it is mainly used for storing files in Amazon S3 and is not optimized for NFS workloads like Amazon FSx File Gateway. It may not provide the same level of performance and features needed for full NFS-based workloads.

我认为这个问题有点超出本次考试范围，但 AWS S3 文件网关的文档中明确提到了 NFS。你或许也可以选择 C，但我认为 D 是“最佳”答案。A. 磁带网关：用于将数据归档到 Amazon S3 和 Glacier。它并非为 NFS 工作负载或迁 移本地基于文件的工作负载而设计。B. 卷网关：用于块存储卷，可能不是 NFS 文件工作负载的最佳选择。它更专注于 块存储解决方案。C. Amazon FSx 文件网关：提供与 Amazon FSx 文件系统的文件接口，与 Windows 文件服务器兼 容。但是，它可能不是将 NFS 工作负载迁移到 AWS 的最佳解决方案。D. Amazon S3 文件网关：支持与 Amazon S3 的文件接口，并允许使用行业标准文件协议（例如 NFS）在 Amazon S3 中存储和检索对象。它专为将本地基于文件的 应用程序与 Amazon S3 集成而设计，因此适合将 NFS 工作负载迁移到 AWS。
D. Amazon S3 文件网关：支持与 Amazon S3 的文件接口，并允许使用 NFS 等行业标准文件协议在 Amazon S3 中存储和检索对象。它专为需要将本地基于文件的应用程序与 Amazon S3 集成的场景而设计，因此非常 适合将 NFS 工作负载迁移到 AWS。

cn_question: 一家公司正在计划迁移到 AWS 云。该公司正在利用 AWS 云采用框架 (AWS CAF) 的视角来识别其能力差距。 这些识别活动属于云转型之旅的哪个阶段？
en_question: A company is planning its migration to the AWS Cloud. The company is identifying its capability gaps by using the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) perspectives. Which phase of the cloud transformation journey includes these identification activities?
comments: "Align phase focuses on identifying capability gaps across the six AWS CAF perspectives, identifying crossorganizational dependencies, and surfacing stakeholder concerns and challenges." Reference: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoption-framework/yourcloud-transformation-journey.html

In the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF), the "Align" phase focuses on understanding and addressing capability gaps to ensure that the organization is prepared for cloud adoption. This involves assessing current capabilities, identifying gaps, and planning for the necessary changes to achieve alignment with cloud best practices.

The answer is B. Align. The Align phase of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) focuses on aligning your organization's  strategic goals with your cloud strategy. This includes: Identifying capability gaps: Assessing your organization's current capabilities and identifying areas where you need to improve. Developing a cloud strategy: Creating a roadmap for your cloud adoption journey. Defining your target operating model: Determining how you will manage and operate your cloud environment. By aligning your organization's goals with your cloud strategy, you can ensure that your cloud adoption efforts are focused on delivering business value.
“对齐阶段的重点在于识别 AWS CAF 六个维度上的能力差距，识别跨组织依赖关系，并找出利益相关者的关 注点和挑战。” 参考：https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoptionframework/your-cloud-transformation-journey.html
在 AWS 云采用框架 (AWS CAF) 中，“对齐”阶段的重点在于了解并解决能力差距，以确保组织为云采用做好 准备。这包括评估现有能力、识别差距，并规划必要的变更，以实现与云最佳实践的一致性。

cn_question: 一家社交媒体公司希望保护其Web应用程序免受常见的Web攻击，例如SQL注入和跨站脚本攻击。 哪项AWS服务可以满足这些要求？
en_question: A social media company wants to protect its web application from common web exploits such as SQL injections and cross-site scripting. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
comments: "AWS WAF is a web application firewall that helps protect web applications from attacks by allowing you to configure rules that allow, block, or monitor (count) web requests based on conditions that you define. These conditions include IP addresses, HTTP headers, HTTP body, URI strings, SQL injection and cross-site scripting." References: https://aws.amazon.com/waf/faqs/

A. Amazon Inspector - security assessment service that helps improve the security and compliance of applications deployed on AWS but doesn't protect web apps from live exploits. B. AWS WAF - A web application firewall that allows users to create custom rules to filter and monitor HTTP or HTTPS requests to a web application. It helps protect against web exploits, including SQL injection attacks. C. Amazon GuardDuty - continuously detects threats and unauthorized behavior but doesn't actively block web exploits. D. Amazon CloudWatch - Primarily for monitoring and managing resources its not a security tool for blocking attacks.
AWS WAF 是一款 Web 应用程序防火墙，它允许您配置规则，根据您定义的条件允许、阻止或监控（计数） Web 请求，从而帮助保护 Web 应用程序免受攻击。这些条件包括 IP 地址、HTTP 标头、HTTP 正文、URI 字符串、 SQL 注入和跨站脚本攻击。参考资料：https://aws.amazon.com/waf/faqs/

A. Amazon Inspector - 安全评估服务，有助于提升部署在 AWS 上的应用程序的安全性和合规性，但无法保 护 Web 应用程序免受实时攻击。B. AWS WAF - Web 应用程序防火墙，允许用户创建自定义规则来过滤和监控发送到 Web 应用程序的 HTTP 或 HTTPS 请求。它有助于防御 Web 漏洞利用，包括 SQL 注入攻击。C. Amazon GuardDuty - 持续检测威胁和未经授权的行为，但不会主动阻止 Web 漏洞利用。D. Amazon CloudWatch - 主要用于监控和管理 资源，并非用于阻止攻击的安全工具。
“AWS WAF 是一款 Web 应用程序防火墙，它允许您配置规则，根据您定义的条件允许、阻止或监控（计数） Web 请求，从而帮助保护 Web 应用程序免受攻击。这些条件包括 IP 地址、HTTP 标头、HTTP 正文、URI 字符串、 SQL 注入和跨站脚本攻击。”

Amazon Machine Image (AMI) - is not a service, it's the image used to launch EC2 instances. C. AWS Launch Wizard is a tool that helps you easily set up complex applications on AWS—like SQL Server, SAP. Not for building or managing EC2 images. D. AWS Elastic Beanstalk is a service for deploying and scaling web applications and services. It's not for building EC2 images.
A. EC2 Image Builder simplifies the creation, maintenance, validation, sharing, and deployment of Linux or Windows images for use with Amazon EC2 and on-premises."

A Launch Wizard then identifies the right AWS resources, such as EC2 instances and EBS volumes, to deploy and run your application. Launch Wizard provides an estimated cost of deployment, and lets you modify your resources to instantly view an updated cost assessment. Once you approve the AWS resources, Launch Wizard automatically provisions and configures the selected resources to create a fully-functioning, production-ready application. AWS Launch Wizard also creates CloudFormation templates that can serve as a baseline to accelerate subsequent deployments. AWS Elastic Beanstalk is a service designed to simplify the deployment and management of web applications  and services. It allows developers to focus on writing code without worrying about the underlying infrastructure. Elastic Beanstalk handles the details of capacity provisioning, load balancing, scaling, and application health monitoring

cn_question: 一家公司每周需要进行一次数据处理，通常需要大约 5 个小时才能完成。 该公司应该使用哪项 AWS 服务来处理这项工作负载？

comments: EC2. AWS Lambda is a serverless computing service that runs your code without provisioning or managing servers. However, Lambda functions have a maximum execution time of 15 minutes. Therefore, Lambda is not suitable for workloads that need to run for longer than 15 minutes. Amazon EC2 is a scalable computing service that provides instances with varying capabilities and pricing options. You can choose an EC2 instance that is right for your data processing job and then terminate the instance when the job is complete. This will help you to save money on your AWS bill.
thank you so much for the link

cn_question: [AI翻译] 一家公司计划在 AWS 上部署容器。该公司希望完全控制托管容器的计算资源。哪项 AWS 服务可以满足这些需求？

cn_question: [AI翻译] 哪项 AWS 服务或功能允许用户创建新的 AWS 账户、将多个账户分组以组织工作流程，并将策略应用于账户组？
en_question: Which AWS service or feature allows users to create new AWS accounts, group multiple accounts to organize workflows, and apply policies to groups of accounts?
comments: Using AWS Organizations, you can programmatically create new AWS accounts and allocate resources, group accounts to organize your workflows, apply policies to accounts or groups for governance, and simplify billing by using a single payment method for all of your accounts.
AWS Organizations

D. AWS Organizations AWS Organizations helps you centrally govern your environment as you scale your workloads on AWS. Whether you are a growing startup or a large enterprise, Organizations helps you to programmatically create new accounts and allocate resources, simplify billing by setting up a single payment method for all of your accounts, create groups of accounts to organize your workflows, and apply policies to these groups for governance. In addition, AWS Organizations is integrated with other AWS services so you can define central configurations, security mechanisms, and resource sharing across accounts in your organization.
D. AWS Organizations - key is multiple accounts

cn_question: 一家公司希望使用行业标准的文件系统协议，在 Amazon S3 中存储和检索其现有本地应用程序的文件。 哪项 AWS 服务能够满足这些要求？
en_question: A company wants to store and retrieve files in Amazon S3 for its existing on-premises applications by using industry-standard file system protocols. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
comments: A. AWS DataSync: is a service for transferring large amounts of data between on-premises storage and Amazon S3, but it is designed for data movement, not for providing real-time file system access B. AWS Snowball Edge: Device that provids large volumes or edge computing offline data transfer capabilities. Not for access to S3 C. Amazon S3 File Gateway: Allows storing and retrieving files in Amazon S3 using industry-standard file protocols. It acts as a bridge between your on-premesis file-based applications and Amazon S3 object storage D. AWS Transfer Family: Fully managed support for SFTP, FTPS and FTP. provides fully managed support for file transfers directly into and out of Amazon S3 or Amazon EFS. Supports file transfer protocols, not file system protocols

The Amazon S3 File Gateway enables you to store and retrieve objects in Amazon Simple Storage Service (S3) using file protocols such as Network File System (NFS) and Server Message Block (SMB). Objects written through S3 File Gateway can be directly accessed in S3.

C. Amazon S3 File Gateway A S3 File Gateway simplifies file storage in Amazon S3, integrates to existing applications through industrystandard file system protocols, and provides a cost-effective alternative to on-premises storage. It also provides low-latency access to data through transparent local caching. A S3 File Gateway manages data transfer to and from AWS, buffers applications from network congestion, optimizes and streams data in  parallel, and manages bandwidth consumption.

A. AWS DataSync：是一项用于在本地存储和 Amazon S3 之间传输大量数据的服务，但它专为数据移动而 设计，而非提供实时文件系统访问。B. AWS Snowball Edge：提供大容量或边缘计算离线数据传输功能的设备。不用 于访问 S3。C. Amazon S3 File Gateway：允许使用行业标准文件协议在 Amazon S3 中存储和检索文件。它充当本 地基于文件的应用程序和 Amazon S3 对象存储之间的桥梁。D. AWS Transfer Family：完全托管支持 SFTP、FTPS 和 FTP。提供对直接进出 Amazon S3 或 Amazon EFS 的文件传输的完全托管支持。支持文件传输协议，而非文件系 统协议。
Amazon S3 文件网关使您能够使用网络文件系统 (NFS) 和服务器消息块 (SMB) 等文件协议在 Amazon Simple Storage Service (S3) 中存储和检索对象。通过 S3 文件网关写入的对象可以直接在 S3 中访问。

cn_question: 一家公司想要阻止 SQL 注入攻击。 该公司应该使用哪项 AWS 服务或功能来满足此需求？
en_question: A company wants to block SQL injection attacks. Which AWS service or feature should the company use to meet this requirement?
comments: SQL ATTACKS- AWS WAF DDOS- AWS SHEILD

A. AWS WAF: web application firewall that allows users to create custom rules to filter and monitor HTTP or HTTPS requests to a web application. It helps protect against common web exploits, including SQL injection attacks B. Network ACLs: Are used to control traffic at the subnet level. C. Security groups: Control inbound and outbound traffic at the network level D. AWS Certificate Manager (ACM): Manages SSL/TLS certificates or encrypting data in transit.

AWS WAF helps protects your website from common attack techniques like SQL injection and Cross-Site Scripting (XSS). In addition, you can create rules that can block or rate-limit traffic from specific user-agents, from specific IP addresses, or that contain particular request headers.
SQL攻击-AWS WAF DDoS攻击-AWS Shield

A. AWS WAF：Web 应用程序防火墙，允许用户创建自定义规则来过滤和监控发送到 Web 应用程序的 HTTP 或 HTTPS 请求。它有助于防御常见的 Web 攻击，包括 SQL 注入攻击。B. 网络访问控制列表 (ACL)：用于在子网级 别控制流量。C. 安全组：在网络级别控制入站和出站流量。D. AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)：管理 SSL/TLS 证书 或加密传输中的数据。

cn_question: 一家公司需要一个统一的工具，以提供与 AWS 服务交互的一致方法。 哪个 AWS 服务或工具可以满足此要求？
en_question: A company wants a unified tool to provide a consistent method to interact with AWS services. Which AWS service or tool will meet this requirement?
comments: "The AWS Command Line Interface (AWS CLI) is a unified tool to manage your AWS services. With just one tool to download and configure, you can control multiple AWS services from the command line and automate them through scripts." Reference: https://aws.amazon.com/cli/

A. AWS CLI: tool that provides a way to interact multiple AWS services from the command line. B. Amazon ECS: A container orchestration service. It manages containerized applications, not for interacting with AWS services C. AWS Cloud9: A cloud IDE D. AWS VPN: used to securely connect on-premises networks to AWS, not to interact with or manage AWS services.

A. The AWS Command Line Interface (AWS CLI) is a unified tool to manage your AWS services. With just one tool to download and configure, you can control multiple AWS services from the command line and automate them through scripts."
AWS 命令​行界面 (AWS CLI) 是一个用于管理 AWS 服务的统一工具。只需下载并配置一个工具，即可通过命 令行控制多个 AWS 服务，并通过脚本实现自动化。参考：https://aws.amazon.com/cli/
A. AWS CLI：一种通过命令行与多个 AWS 服务交互的工具。B. Amazon ECS：一种容器编排服务。它用于 管理容器化应用程序，而非与 AWS 服务交互。C. AWS Cloud9：一个云集成开发环境 (IDE)。D. AWS VPN：用于将 本地网络安全地连接到 AWS，而非与 AWS 服务交互或管理 AWS 服务。

cn_question: 一家公司需要评估其 AWS 环境，并从成本、性能、服务限制、容错性和安全性五个方面提供最佳实践建议。 该公司可以使用哪些 AWS 服务来满足这些要求？
en_question: A company needs to evaluate its AWS environment and provide best practice recommendations in five categories: cost, performance, service limits, fault tolerance and security. Which AWS service can the company use to meet these requirements?
comments: A. AWS Shield: Protects against DDoS attacks. B. AWS WAF – Protects web applications from common exploits like SQL injection C. AWS Trusted Advisor: provides real-time recommendations across five key categories: Cost, Performance, Service limits, Fault tolerance, Security D. AWS Service Catalog – Manages approved AWS resources for use within an organization.: Manages approved AWS resources for use within an organization.
C. AWS Trusted Advisor Analyze your AWS accounts and provides recommendation on 5 categories • Cost optimization • Performance • Security • Fault tolerance • Service limits
who gives reccomendations its trusted advisor always
A. AWS Shield：防御 DDoS 攻击。B. AWS WAF：保护 Web 应用程序免受 SQL 注入等常见攻击。C. AWS Trusted Advisor：提供涵盖成本、性能、服务限制、容错性和安全性五个关键类别的实时建议。D. AWS Service Catalog：管理组织内部使用的已批准 AWS 资源。
C. AWS Trusted Advisor 分析您的 AWS 账户，并就以下 5 个方面提供建议：• 成本优化 • 性能 • 安全性 • 容错能力 • 服务限制

cn_question: AWS 云采用框架 (AWS CAF) 中的哪个视角包含了配置管理和补丁管理功能？
en_question: Which perspective in the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) includes capabilities for configuration management and patch management?
comments: AWS CAF Operations perspective capabilities • Observability • Event management (AIOps) • Incident and problem management • Change and release management • Performance and capacity management • Configuration management • Patch management • Availability and continuity management • Application management Reference: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/whitepapers/latest/overview-aws-cloud-adoptionframework/operations-perspective.html
Opetations is the correct answer.

cn_question: 一家公司的计算工作负载稳定、可预测且不间断。 哪些亚马逊 EC2 实例购买选项能够以最具成本效益的方式满足这些要求？（选择两个。）
en_question: A company has a compute workload that is steady, predictable, and uninterruptible. Which Amazon EC2 instance purchasing options meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively? (Choose two.)
comments: B. Reserved Instances: Reserved Instances provide a significant discount compared to On-Demand Instances in exchange for a commitment to a one- or three-year term. Since the workload is steady and predictable, you can forecast your usage and purchase Reserved Instances accordingly, optimizing costs over time. D. Saving Plans: Similar to Reserved Instances, Savings Plans offer significant discounts on usage in exchange for committing to a specific amount of compute usage (measured in dollars per hour) over a one- or three-year term. Savings Plans provide flexibility across a wider range of instance types and regions compared to Reserved Instances.
Yes the answer is BD.
Not dedicated hosts - keywords "MOST cost-effective"
B. 预留实例：预留实例相比按需实例提供大幅折扣，但需承诺一年或三年的使用期限。由于工作负载稳定且 可预测，您可以预测使用量并据此购买预留实例，从而优化长期成本。D. 节能计划：与预留实例类似，节能计划也提 供大幅折扣，但需承诺在一年或三年内达到特定的计算使用量（以美元/小时为单位）。与预留实例相比，节能计划在更 广泛的实例类型和区域范围内提供更大的灵活性。

cn_question: 在AWS责任共担模型下，AWS与其客户之间共同承担的责任是什么？

cn_options.A: Amazon EC2 实例操作系统配置
cn_options.B: 应用文件系统服务器端加密

en_question: Which option is a shared responsibility between AWS and its customers under the AWS shared responsibility model?

en_options.A: Configuration of Amazon EC2 instance operating systems

comments: Shared Controls "AWS is responsible for patching and fixing flaws within the infrastructure, but customers are responsible for patching their guest OS and applications." Reference: https://aws.amazon.com/compliance/shared-responsibility-model/

A. Configuration of Amazon EC2 instance operating systems This is customer responsibility because you manage and configure the OS on your EC2 instances. B. Application file system server-side encryption This is generally customer responsibility, as customers configure encryption for their data and applications. C. Patch management This is shared responsibility: AWS patches the underlying infrastructure and managed services. Customers are responsible for patching the guest OS and applications running on EC2 or other IaaS. D. Security of the physical infrastructure This is AWS responsibility — AWS manages data centers, physical hardware, and network infrastructure.

Under the AWS shared responsibility model, patch management is a shared responsibility. AWS is responsible for patching the infrastructure and services they manage, while customers are responsible for patching the operating systems and applications running on their Amazon EC2 instances and other resources.
共享控制权：“AWS 负责修补基础设施中的缺陷，但客户负责修补其客户操作系统和应用程序。” 参考：

A. Amazon EC2 实例操作系统配置：这是客户的责任，因为您需要管理和配置 EC2 实例上的操作系统。B. 应用程序文件系统服务器端加密：这通常是客户的责任，因为客户需要为其数据和应用程序配置加密。C. 补丁管理： 这是双方共同的责任：AWS 负责修补底层基础设施和托管服务。客户负责修补运行在 EC2 或其他 IaaS 上的客户操作 系统和应用程序。D. 物理基础设施安全：这是 AWS 的责任——AWS 管理数据中心、物理硬件和网络基础设施。

cn_question: 一家公司希望将其本地工作负载迁移到 AWS 云。该公司希望将工作负载分离，以便向不同部门收取费用。 哪些 AWS 服务或功能可以满足这些要求？（选择两项。）
en_question: A company wants to migrate its on-premises workloads to the AWS Cloud. The company wants to separate workloads for chargeback to different departments. Which AWS services or features will meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
comments: B. Consolidated Billing Consolidated billing is a feature of AWS Organizations that allows you to combine billing for multiple AWS accounts. With consolidated billing, each department can have its own AWS account for tracking and managing its resources separately, while the company enjoys the convenience of a single payment method for all these accounts. This setup makes it easier to track costs and implement chargeback mechanisms. E. Multiple AWS Accounts Creating multiple AWS accounts, one for each department, is an effective way to segregate resources, manage permissions, and track costs separately. This approach enhances security by limiting the scope of access and impact of changes, and it simplifies the process of attributing costs to the right department. Combined with consolidated billing, it provides both organizational flexibility and centralized financial control.
security and access wise, delegation is one of the benefits of creating multiple aws accounts

B. Consolidated billing: AWS Consolidated Billing allows organizations to manage multiple AWS accounts under a single AWS Organization. It simplifies the billing process by consolidating usage and billing into one bill while still tracking  costs for each account. This enables chargeback to specific departments based on their resource usage. E. Multiple AWS accounts: Using multiple AWS accounts helps isolate workloads for different departments or teams. This makes it easier to allocate costs directly to specific accounts, enabling precise chargeback. Other Options:

B. 合并账单 合并账单是 AWS Organizations 的一项功能，允许您将多个 AWS 账户的账单合并在一起。通过 合并账单，每个部门都可以拥有自己的 AWS 账户，用于单独跟踪和管理其资源，而公司则可以享受所有账户使用单一 支付方式的便利。这种设置使成本跟踪和实施费用分摊机制更加便捷。E. 多个 AWS 账户 为每个部门创建多个 AWS 账 户，是隔离资源、管理权限和单独跟踪成本的有效方法。这种方法通过限制访问范围和变更影响来增强安全性，并简化 了将成本归因于正确部门的过程。结合合并账单，它既能提供组织灵活性，又能实现集中式财务控制。
从安全性和访问权限的角度来看，委托是创建多个 AWS 账户的优势之一。

cn_question: 根据AWS责任共担模型，哪些任务是AWS的责任？

cn_options.A: 对存储在 Amazon S3 中的对象启用客户端加密。
cn_options.B: 配置 IAM 安全策略，使其符合最小权限原则。
cn_options.C: 修补 Amazon EC2 实例上的客户操作系统。
cn_options.D: 对 Nitro Hypervisor 应用更新。

en_question: Which task is a responsibility of AWS, according to the AWS shared responsibility model?

en_options.A: Enable client-side encryption for objects that are stored in Amazon S3.
en_options.B: Configure IAM security policies to comply with the principle of least privilege.
en_options.C: Patch the guest operating system on an Amazon EC2 instance.
en_options.D: Apply updates to the Nitro Hypervisor.

comments: Given the options: A. Enabling client-side encryption for objects that are stored in Amazon S3 is a customer responsibility. This involves managing encryption keys and configuring encryption settings. B. Configuring IAM (Identity and Access Management) security policies to comply with the principle of least privilege is a customer responsibility. It involves managing access and permissions for AWS resources. C. Patching the guest operating system on an Amazon EC2 instance is a customer responsibility. It involves maintaining the security of the operating system running on the EC2 instance. D. Applying updates to the Nitro Hypervisor is an AWS responsibility. The Nitro Hypervisor is a component of the underlying infrastructure managed by AWS. Therefore, the correct answer is: D. Apply updates to the Nitro Hypervisor.
D. Applying updates to the Nitro Hypervisor is an AWS responsibility. The Nitro Hypervisor is a component of the underlying infrastructure managed by AWS.
A, B, and C related to data that should be responsible by the customer.

选项如下：A. 为存储在 Amazon S3 中的对象启用客户端加密是客户的责任。这涉及管理加密密钥和配置加 密设置。B. 配置 IAM（身份和访问管理）安全策略以符合最小权限原则是客户的责任。这涉及管理 AWS 资源的访问权 限。C. 为 Amazon EC2 实例上的客户操作系统打补丁是客户的责任。这涉及维护 EC2 实例上运行的操作系统的安全 性。D. 对 Nitro Hypervisor 应用更新是 AWS 的责任。Nitro Hypervisor 是 AWS 管理的底层基础设施的一个组件。因 此，正确答案是：D. 对 Nitro Hypervisor 应用更新。
件。 D. 对 Nitro Hypervisor 进行更新是 AWS 的职责。Nitro Hypervisor 是 AWS 管理的底层基础设施的一个组

cn_question: 一家公司希望将单体应用现代化改造为微服务架构，并计划将应用迁移到 AWS 云平台。 该公司应该采用哪种迁移策略？
en_question: A company wants to modernize and convert a monolithic application into microservices. The company wants to move the application to AWS. Which migration strategy should the company use?
comments: It should be D. Refactor: Lift and shift—moving applications to the cloud as-is. This is also sometimes referred to as rehosting. Refactor —modifying applications to better support the cloud environment.Replatform—moving applications to the cloud without major changes, but taking advantage of benefits of the cloud environment.Rebuild—rewrite the application from scratch.Replace—retire the application and replace it with a new cloud-native application.
is it D? can anyone confirm?

D. Refactor (also called re-architect) is the migration strategy where an application is redesigned and modified to take full advantage of modern cloud-native features, such as microservices architectures. In this approach, a monolithic application is broken down into smaller, loosely coupled services (microservices) to improve scalability, flexibility, and maintainability. Why Refactor is the Best Choice: Breaking a monolithic application into microservices typically requires significant changes to the application's architecture and codebase. Refactoring ensures the application leverages cloud-native features like containerization (using AWS services like Amazon ECS, Amazon EKS, or AWS Fargate) and serverless components (e.g., AWS Lambda).  It aligns with the goal of modernization and preparing the application for long-term agility and scalability in the cloud.

应该是 D. 重构：直接迁移（Lift and shift）——将应用程序原封不动地迁移到云端。这有时也称为重新托管 （rehosting）。重构（Refactor）——修改应用程序以更好地支持云环境。平台重构（Replatform）——在不进行重大 更改的情况下将应用程序迁移到云端，但充分利用云环境的优势。重建（Rebuild）——从头开始重写应用程序。替换 （Replace）——停用现有应用程序并用新的云原生应用程序替换它。

D. 重构（也称为重新架构）是一种迁移策略，它通过重新设计和修改应用程序，使其能够充分利用现代云原 生特性，例如微服务架构。在这种方法中，单体应用程序被拆分成更小、松耦合的服务（微服务），从而提高可扩展 性、灵活性和可维护性。为什么重构是最佳选择：将单体应用程序拆分成微服务通常需要对应用程序的架构和代码库进 行重大更改。重构确保应用程序能够利用云原生特性，例如容器化（使用 AWS 服务，如 Amazon ECS、Amazon EKS 或 AWS Fargate）和无服务器组件（例如 AWS Lambda）。它符合现代化目标，并使应用程序能够在云端实现长期的 敏捷性和可扩展性。

cn_question: [AI翻译] 一家公司正在将本地数据中心迁移到 AWS 云。该公司必须以最少的运营开销将 50 PB 的文件存储数据迁移到 AWS。该公司应使用哪项 AWS 服务或资源来满足这些需求？
en_question: A company is moving an on-premises data center to the AWS Cloud. The company must migrate 50 petabytes of file storage data to AWS with the least possible operational overhead. Which AWS service or resource should the company use to meet these requirements?
comments: AWS Snowmobile is the appropriate choice for migrating 50 petabytes of file storage data to AWS with the least possible operational overhead. Snowmobile is a data transfer service that physically transports massive amounts of data to the AWS Cloud. It is a secure and efficient solution for extremely large data transfers.

A. AWS Snowmobile is specifically designed for transferring large-scale data (up to 100 petabytes) from onpremises data centers to AWS. It is a physical data transport solution where a secure truck (essentially a data center on wheels) is used to transport massive amounts of data. This method minimizes operational overhead and is highly efficient for very large data migrations like the 50 petabytes mentioned. Other Options: B. AWS Snowball Edge: While AWS Snowball Edge is suitable for transferring large amounts of data (up to tens of terabytes per device), it would require hundreds of devices to transfer 50 petabytes, resulting in significantly more operational overhead compared to Snowmobile.
Snow edge is in TBs where as snow mobile is in PB

en_question: Which of the following is a recommended design principle for AWS Cloud architecture?

en_options.B: Build a single application component that can handle all the application functionality.
en_options.C: Make large changes on fewer iterations to reduce chances of failure.
en_options.D: Avoid monolithic architecture by segmenting workloads.

comments: Avoid monolithic architecture

D. Avoid monolithic architecture by segmenting workloads. In AWS Cloud architecture, one of the key design principles is to avoid monolithic architectures and instead use a microservices or segmented approach for workloads. This promotes flexibility, scalability, and maintainability by breaking down applications into smaller, independent components. This makes it easier to deploy, scale, and manage applications, and it improves fault isolation.
This is not a recommended design principle because it goes against the principles of building scalable, resilient, and maintainable cloud-based systems.
D. 通过工作负载分段避免单体架构。在 AWS 云架构中，关键设计原则之一是避免单体架构，而是采用微服 务或分段式工作负载方法。这种方法将应用程序分解为更小、更独立的组件，从而提升灵活性、可扩展性和可维护性。 这使得应用程序的部署、扩展和管理更加便捷，并增强了故障隔离能力。 轰炸机 两年前 为什么不是A？
这不是推荐的设计原则，因为它违背了构建可扩展、有弹性和可维护的云系统的原则。 BoBOMr 两年前 D. 通过划分工作负载来避免单体架构。单体架构可能变得笨重、难以维护且容易出现故障。将工作负载划分 为更小、更易于管理的组件，可以提高云环境中的可扩展性、弹性和灵活性。这种方法符合现代云原生架构的最佳实 践，即服务解耦、可独立部署且可独立扩展。

cn_question: AWS 的哪项服务可以帮助用户审核其 AWS 账户中的 API 活动？
en_question: Which AWS service helps users audit API activity across their AWS account?
comments: A. AWS CloudTrail: A service that records API calls and related events for your AWS account. B. Amazon Inspector is designed to help assess applications for vulnerabilities and deviations from best practices, but it does not audit API activity. C. AWS WAF: Protects web applications from common web exploits D. AWS Config - is used for assessing, auditing, and evaluating the configurations of your AWS resources. but it's more focused on resource configuration states rather than API activity monitoring.
A. AWS CloudTrail

With AWS CloudTrail, you can monitor your AWS deployments in the cloud by getting a history of AWS API calls for your account, including API calls made by using the AWS Management Console, the AWS SDKs, the command line tools, and higher-level AWS services. You can also identify which users and accounts called AWS APIs for services that support CloudTrail, the source IP address from which the calls were made, and when the calls occurred. You can integrate CloudTrail into applications using the API, automate trail creation for your organization, check the status of your trails, and control how administrators turn CloudTrail logging on  and off.
A. AWS CloudTrail：一项用于记录 AWS 账户 API 调用及相关事件的服务。B. Amazon Inspector 旨在帮助 评估应用程序的漏洞和与最佳实践的偏差，但它不审计 API 活动。C. AWS WAF：保护 Web 应用程序免受常见 Web 攻击。D. AWS Config：用于评估、审计和审核 AWS 资源的配置，但它更侧重于资源配置状态，而非 API 活动监控。

借助 AWS CloudTrail，您可以监控云端 AWS 部署，获取账户的 AWS API 调用历史记录，包括使用 AWS 管 理控制台、AWS 开发工具包、命令行工具以及更高级别 AWS 服务发起的 API 调用。您还可以识别哪些用户和账户调 用了支持 CloudTrail 的 AWS API 服务、调用来源的 IP 地址以及调用发生的时间。您可以使用 API 将 CloudTrail 集成 到应用程序中，自动创建组织的跟踪记录，检查跟踪记录的状态，并控制管理员如何启用和禁用 CloudTrail 日志记 录。https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cloudtrail/

cn_question: 根据 AWS 责任共担模型，客户应承担哪些任务？

en_question: ’ Which task is a customer s responsibility, according to the AWS shared responsibility model?

en_options.A: Management of the guest operating systems
en_options.B: Maintenance of the configuration of infrastructure devices
en_options.C: Management of the host operating systems and virtualization
en_options.D: Maintenance of the software that powers Availability Zones

comments: Management of the guest operating system

The correct answer is: A. Management of the guest operating systems. Explanation: Under the AWS shared responsibility model, AWS is responsible for the security of the cloud (the infrastructure, hardware, and software that supports AWS services), while the customer is responsible for security in the cloud (the configuration and security of their own applications, data, and services). The customer is responsible for managing their guest operating systems, which are the operating systems they install and manage on their EC2 instances. This includes ensuring the operating system is updated, patched, and secured.
Agree with A as the right answer
正确答案是：A. 客户操作系统管理。解释：根据 AWS 的责任共担模型，AWS 负责云的安全（支持 AWS 服 务的基础设施、硬件和软件），而客户负责云内的安全（其自身应用程序、数据和服务的配置和安全）。客户负责管理其 客户操作系统，即客户在其 EC2 实例上安装和管理的操作系统。这包括确保操作系统得到更新、打补丁和加固。

It can add or remove instances based on predefined conditions, ensuring that you have the right number of instances to handle varying workloads, while optimizing costs.
D. Amazon EC2 自动扩展 Amazon EC2 自动扩展是一项服务，可让您根据不断变化的需求自动调整应用程 序中 Amazon EC2 实例的数量。它可以根据预定义的条件添加或删除实例，确保您拥有足够数量的实例来处理不同的 工作负载，同时优化成本。

cn_question: 用户希望安全地自动管理和轮换应用程序之间共享的凭证，同时尽可能减少管理任务所需的时间。 可以使用哪项 AWS 服务或功能来实现此目的？

cn_options.B: AWS 密钥管理服务 (AWS KMS)

en_question: A user wants to securely automate the management and rotation of credentials that are shared between applications, while spending the least amount of time on managing tasks. Which AWS service or feature can be used to accomplish this?
comments: AWS Secrets Manager helps in the secure storage, rotation, and retrieval of sensitive credentials such as API keys, database passwords, and other secrets used by applications. AWS CloudHSM provides hardware-based key storage for cryptographic keys and is more focused on custom key storage and compliance needs, which might be an overkill for just credential management and rotation. ’ AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) is used for creating and managing encryption keys. While it's crucial for encryption, it doesn t directly handle the automated management and rotation of credentials shared between applications.
Answer is C AWS Secrets Manager

A. AWS CloudHSM: AWS CloudHSM (Hardware Security Module) is a service that provides hardware-based key storage for sensitive data and cryptographic operations. B. AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) is used for creating and controlling encryption keys used to encrypt your data C. AWS Secrets Manager is designed specifically for storing, managing, and rotating credentials securely and automatically D. Server-side encryption process where data is encrypted at the server level before it is stored, which is primarily used to protect data at rest

AWS Secrets Manager 有助于安全地存储、轮换和检索敏感凭证，例如 API 密钥、数据库密码以及应用程序 使用的其他密钥。AWS CloudHSM 提供基于硬件的密钥存储，用于存储加密密钥，更侧重于自定义密钥存储和合规性 需求，对于仅用于凭证管理和轮换而言可能过于复杂。AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) 用于创建和管理 加密密钥。虽然它对加密至关重要，但它并不直接处理应用程序之间共享凭证的自动管理和轮换。
答案是 C AWS Secrets Manager

B. Amazon Macie Amazon Macie 使用机器学习来自动发现、分类和保护 AWS 环境中的敏感数据，例如个 人身份信息 (PII) 和知识产权。

cn_question: 对于 AWS 账户根用户而言，哪些操作是最佳实践？（选择两项。）

cn_options.A: 与团队成员共享 root 用户凭据。
cn_options.B: 为该帐户创建多个 root 用户，并按环境进行区分。
cn_options.C: 对 root 用户启用多因素身份验证 (MFA)。
cn_options.D: 创建一个具有管理员权限的 IAM 用户来执行日常管理任务，而不是使用 root 用户。
cn_options.E: 使用程序化访问而不是 root 用户和密码。

en_question: Which actions are best practices for an AWS account root user? (Choose two.)

en_options.A: Share root user credentials with team members.
en_options.B: Create multiple root users for the account, separated by environment.
en_options.C: Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) on the root user.
en_options.D: Create an IAM user with administrator privileges for daily administrative tasks, instead of using the root user.
en_options.E: Use programmatic access instead of the root user and password.

comments: C. Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) on the root user: Enabling MFA adds an extra layer of security to the root user account. Even if someone gains access to the root user credentials, they would also need the second factor (such as a phone or hardware token) to access the account. D. Create an IAM user with administrator privileges for daily administrative tasks, instead of using the root user: It is a best practice to avoid using the root user for day-to-day tasks because the root user has unrestricted access to all resources and can make significant changes to your account. Instead, you should create an IAM user with administrator privileges and use that for routine management tasks.

The best practices for an AWS account root user are: C. Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) on the root user. Enabling MFA adds an extra layer of security to protect the root user account from unauthorized access. D. Create an IAM user with administrator privileges for daily administrative tasks, instead of using the root user. It's recommended to create an IAM user with the necessary permissions for daily administrative tasks rather than using the root user. This practice helps in maintaining better security and accountability by reducing the reliance on the highly privileged root user account.  So, the correct options are C and D
CD are correct

C. 为根用户启用多因素身份验证 (MFA)：启用 MFA 可为根用户帐户增加一层额外的安全保障。即使有人获 得了根用户的凭据，他们也需要第二个因素（例如手机或硬件令牌）才能访问该帐户。D. 创建一个具有管理员权限的 IAM 用户来执行日常管理任务，而不是使用根用户：最佳实践是避免使用根用户执行日常任务，因为根用户拥有对所有 资源的无限制访问权限，并且可以对您的帐户进行重大更改。相反，您应该创建一个具有管理员权限的 IAM 用户，并 使用该用户执行例行管理任务。

AWS 账户根用户的最佳实践是：C. 为根用户启用多因素身份验证 (MFA)。启用 MFA 可以增加一层额外的安 全保护，防止根用户账户遭受未经授权的访问。D. 创建一个具有管理员权限的 IAM 用户来执行日常管理任务，而不是 使用根用户。建议创建一个具有必要权限的 IAM 用户来执行日常管理任务，而不是使用根用户。这种做法有助于通过 减少对高权限根用户账户的依赖来维护更好的安全性和问责制。因此，正确选项是 C 和 D。

en_question: A company is running a critical workload on an Amazon RDS DB instance. The company needs the DB instance to be highly available with a recovery time of less than 5 minutes. Which solution will meet these requirements?

en_options.C: Take frequent snapshots of the DB instance. Store the snapshots in Amazon S3.
en_options.D: Modify the DB instance to be a Multi-AZ deployment.

comments: In an Amazon RDS Multi-AZ deployment, Amazon RDS automatically creates a primary database (DB) instance and synchronously replicates the data to an instance in a different AZ. When it detects a failure, Amazon RDS automatically fails over to a standby instance without manual intervention.
Keyword: Highly Available
在 Amazon RDS 多可用区部署中，Amazon RDS 会自动创建一个主数据库 (DB) 实例，并将数据同步复制到 另一个可用区中的实例。当检测到故障时，Amazon RDS 会自动故障转移到备用实例，无需人工干预。

cn_question: 一家公司需要在 Amazon S3 存储桶和本地应用程序之间传输数据。 根据 AWS 责任共担模型，谁负责这些数据的安全？

The company According to the AWS shared responsibility model, the customer is responsible for the security of data in transit between AWS services (such as an Amazon S3 bucket) and their on-premises applications. This includes implementing encryption, secure transfer protocols (e.g., HTTPS or SFTP), and network configurations to ensure data security.
Networking and traffic protection are a customer responsibility. And of course Identity and Access Management which are needed when accessing an S3 bucket

网络和流量保护是客户的责任。当然，访问 S3 存储桶时也需要身份和访问管理。

cn_question: 可以使用哪项 AWS 服务按需检索合规性报告？
en_question: Which AWS service can be used to retrieve compliance reports on demand?

B: AWS Artifact provides on-demand access to AWS compliance reports, such as ISO, SOC, and PCI reports. C: AWS Security Hub gives a central view of security findings but does not provide compliance reports. D: AWS Certificate Manager manages SSL/TLS certificates, not compliance documentation.

A：AWS Secrets Manager 用于安全地存储和轮换数据库凭证和 API 密钥等机密信息，而非用于生成合规性 报告。B：AWS Artifact 提供对 AWS 合规性报告（例如 ISO、SOC 和 PCI 报告）的按需访问。C：AWS Security Hub 提供安全发现的集中视图，但不提供合规性报告。D：AWS Certificate Manager 管理 SSL/TLS 证书，而非合规 性文档。

cn_question: 一家公司计划迁移到 AWS 云。该公司正在收集其本地基础设施的信息，需要主机名、IP 地址和 MAC 地址等信 息。 哪项 AWS 服务能够满足这些要求？

cn_options.B: AWS 应用程序迁移服务

en_question: A company plans to migrate to the AWS Cloud. The company is gathering information about its onpremises infrastructure and requires information such as the hostname, IP address, and MAC address. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
comments: AWS Application Discovery Service collects both server and database configuration information. Server information includes hostnames, IP addresses, MAC addresses, as well as the resource allocation and utilization details of key resources such as CPU, network, memory, and disk.

’ A: AWS DataSync is used for transferring data between on-premises storage and AWS, not for discovering infrastructure details. B: AWS Application Migration Service is used to migrate applications to AWS but doesn t collect detailed inventory data like hostnames or MAC addresses. C: AWS Application Discovery Service collects configuration, usage, and behavior data from on-premises servers including hostname, IP address, and MAC address—ideal for migration planning. D: AWS DMS is used specifically for migrating databases, not for discovering infrastructure details.

A：AWS DataSync 用于在本地存储和 AWS 之间传输数据，而不是用于发现基础设施详情。B：AWS 应用程 序迁移服务 (Application Migration Service) 用于将应用程序迁移到 AWS，但不会收集主机名或 MAC 地址等详细的 清单数据。C：AWS 应用程序发现服务 (Application Discovery Service) 会从本地服务器收集配置、使用情况和行为 数据，包括主机名、IP 地址和 MAC 地址——非常适合用于迁移规划。D：AWS DMS 专门用于迁移数据库，而不是用 于发现基础设施详情。
C - AWS 应用程序发现服务收集服务器和数据库配置信息

cn_question: 一家公司计划将其应用程序迁移到 AWS 云。 该公司应该使用哪种 AWS 工具或资源集来分析和评估其迁移准备情况？
en_question: A company is planning to migrate its application to the AWS Cloud. Which AWS tool or set of resources should the company use to analyze and assess its readiness for migration?
comments: ’ A: AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) helps organizations assess their readiness for cloud migration by providing guidance across multiple perspectives like business, people, governance, platform, and security. B: AWS Pricing Calculator helps estimate costs but doesn t assess migration readiness. C: AWS Well-Architected Framework helps evaluate the design of workloads already in the cloud, not readiness for migration. D: AWS Budgets tracks and manages costs, not cloud migration preparedness.

A. AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) The AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) is indeed designed to help organizations develop an effective strategy for adopting the AWS Cloud. It provides guidance on key aspects of cloud adoption, including business, people, governance, and technology considerations. While the AWS CAF does not directly offer a tool for analyzing readiness for migration, it does provide a comprehensive framework that can help organizations assess their readiness and plan their migration strategy effectively.
keywords = planning to migrate its application to the AWS Cloud
A：AWS 云采用框架 (AWS CAF) 通过提供业务、人员、治理、平台和安全等多个维度的指导，帮助组织评估 其云迁移准备情况。B：AWS 定价计算器有助于估算成本，但无法评估迁移准备情况。C：AWS 架构完善框架有助于 评估已在云端的工作负载的设计，而非迁移准备情况。D：AWS Budgets 用于跟踪和管理成本，而非云迁移准备情 况。
A. AWS 云采用框架 (AWS CAF) AWS 云采用框架 (AWS CAF) 旨在帮助组织制定有效的 AWS 云采用策略。 它就云采用的关键方面提供指导，包括业务、人员、治理和技术方面的考量。虽然 AWS CAF 本身并不提供用于分析迁 移准备情况的工具，但它提供了一个全面的框架，可以帮助组织评估自身的准备情况并有效地规划迁移策略。

cn_question: 一家公司正在开发一个应用程序，该应用程序需要在应用程序组件之间发送、存储和接收消息。该公司还有另一 个要求，即消息必须按照先进先出 (FIFO) 的顺序处理。 该公司应该使用哪项 AWS 服务？
en_question: A company is building an application that requires the ability to send, store, and receive messages between application components. The company has another requirement to process messages in first-in, first-out (FIFO) order. Which AWS service should the company use?
comments: A: AWS Step Functions is used to coordinate components of distributed applications using workflows, not for message queuing. B: Amazon SNS is a pub/sub messaging service, but it does not guarantee FIFO message processing. C: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams is used for real-time data streaming and analytics, not for general-purpose messaging with strict FIFO. D: Amazon SQS (specifically the FIFO queue type) is designed to send, store, and receive messages in order, ensuring first-in, first-out processing.

Amazon SQS FIFO queues preserve the order in which messages are sent and received, and avoid that a message is processed more than once. This ensures that the messages are processed in first-in, first-out (FIFO) order1.

A：AWS Step Functions 用于通过工作流协调分布式应用程序的各个组件，而不是用于消息队列。B： Amazon SNS 是一种发布/订阅消息服务，但它不保证先进先出 (FIFO) 的消息处理。C：Amazon Kinesis Data Streams 用于实时数据流和分析，而不是用于严格遵循 FIFO 的通用消息传递。D：Amazon SQS（特别是 FIFO 队列 类型）旨在按顺序发送、存储和接收消息，从而确保先进先出处理。
Amazon SQS FIFO 队列保留消息发送和接收的顺序，避免消息被多次处理。这确保了消息按照先进先出 (FIFO) 的顺序进行处理¹。

en_question: Which task is a responsibility of AWS, according to the AWS shared responsibility model?

comments: A. Configure identity and access management for applications- customer responsibility B. Manage encryption options for data that is stored on AWS- customer responsibility C. Configure security groups for Amazon EC2 instances- customer responsibility D. Maintain the physical hardware of the infrastructure- AWS responsibility Correct answer is D
D. Maintain the physical hardware of the infrastructure.

"D. Maintain the physical hardware of the infrastructure" is not the best answer because, according to the AWS shared responsibility model, AWS is responsible for managing the physical infrastructure of the cloud, including data centers and hardware maintenance. However, it's the customer's responsibility to secure and manage the data and applications they deploy on AWS infrastructure. Therefore, option B is the best choice as it aligns with AWS's responsibility for managing encryption options for data stored on their platform.
A. 为应用程序配置身份和访问管理 - 客户责任；B. 管理存储在 AWS 上的数据的加密选项 - 客户责任；C. 为 Amazon EC2 实例配置安全组 - 客户责任；D. 维护基础设施的物理硬件 - AWS 责任。正确答案是 D。
D. 维护基础设施的物理硬件。 底特律人 两年前 “D. 维护基础设施的物理硬件”并非最佳答案，因为根据 AWS 的责任共担模型，AWS 负责管理云的物理基础 设施，包括数据中心和硬件维护。然而，客户有责任保护和管理其部署在 AWS 基础设施上的数据和应用程序。因此， 选项 B 是最佳选择，因为它符合 AWS 管理其平台上存储的数据加密选项的责任。 奥德雷米雷斯 两年前 D 正确

A. AWS Storage Gateway: It provides a hybrid cloud storage solution that allows you to seamlessly integrate on-premises environments with cloud storage. It offers different storage interfaces, including file, volume, and tape, and allows you to cache data locally for frequently accessed information while storing the entire dataset in the AWS Cloud. This setup enables efficient access to data stored in AWS while maintaining a local cache for performance.

comments: tag is a label that you or AWS assigns to an AWS resource.You can use tags to organize your resources or billing purposes.
B. Use tags to organize the resources. Activate cost allocation tags to track AWS costs on a detailed level.

Per Robot: To effectively organize resources and track AWS costs based on business department, environment, and application, the most suitable solution is B. Use tags to organize the resources and activate cost allocation tags. ’ Here s why: Tags for Cost Allocation:  Tags allow you to label your AWS resources with metadata. By assigning relevant tags (such as department, environment, or application) to your resources, you can categorize them effectively. Cost allocation tags specifically help you track costs at a detailed level by associating costs with specific tags. For example, you can tag resources with labels like “Marketing,” “Development,” “Production,” etc. AWS then aggregates costs based on these tags, providing granular insights into spending patterns. This approach allows you to allocate costs accurately and understand which departments, projects, or applications are consuming resources and incurring costs.
标签是您或 AWS 分配给 AWS 资源的标签。您可以使用标签来组织资源或用于计费目的。

comments: AD is across regions BE is across the smaller availability zones
A. Amazon Route 53 and D. AWS Global Accelerator.

A. Amazon Route 53: Amazon Route 53 is a scalable and highly available Domain Name System (DNS) web service. It supports routing policies that enable failover across different AWS Regions, ensuring high availability and low latency for end-users. D. AWS Global Accelerator: AWS Global Accelerator improves the availability and performance of your applications with local or global users. cn_question: 哪种亚马逊 EC2 实例定价模式可以提供高达 90% 的折扣？
en_question: Which Amazon EC2 instance pricing model can provide discounts of up to 90%?
comments: Spot Instances: Get up to 90% discount compared to On demand
D. Spot Instances

D. Spot Instances. The Amazon EC2 instance pricing model that can provide discounts of up to 90% is the "Spot Instances" pricing model. With Spot Instances, you can bid for unused EC2 capacity, and if your bid price exceeds the current Spot price, your instances will be terminated. This model allows you to take advantage of unused capacity at significantly reduced prices compared to On-Demand instances. However, it comes with the caveat that your instances may be terminated if the Spot price increases above your bid price.
竞价型实例：与按需实例相比，最高可享 90% 折扣
D. 竞价型实例。Amazon EC2 实例定价模型中，最高可提供 90% 折扣的是“竞价型实例”定价模型。使用竞 价型实例，您可以竞价购买未使用的 EC2 容量。如果您的出价高于当前的竞价价格，您的实例将被终止。与按需实例 相比，此模型允许您以显著更低的价格利用未使用的容量。但是，需要注意的是，如果竞价价格上涨到高于您的出价， 您的实例可能会被终止。
